headline: Days after heatwave, French government cuts climate change adaptation funds
description: The Green Fund, which helps local authorities finance environmental protection and climate adaptation projects, is facing a €162.5 million cut, a nearly 20% reduction.

headline: Marjane Satrapi, author of 'Persepolis,' dies at 56
Israel and Lebanon agree to implement conditional ceasefire
Thomas Piketty's lab proposes ambitious plan to cut global inequality and curb climate change
description: Building a more equitable world while respecting planetary limits is possible, economists from the World Inequality Lab assert in a report published Thursday. To achieve this, they say, targeted reductions in consumption in specific sectors, a drastic cut in fossil fuel use and changes in dietary habits will be necessary.

headline: Zelensky's rehabilitation of controversial Ukrainian nationalists sparks anger in Israel and Poland
description: By attending a reburial ceremony for Andriy Melnyk, a pro-independence leader who collaborated with Nazi Germany against the Soviet Union, Volodymyr Zelensky has backed the country's evolving memorial politics, which have been focused on opposition to Moscow since the February 2022 invasion.

headline: The past, present and future cinema of director Céline Sciamma
description: From June 3 to 16, the MK2 Bibliothèque movie theater in Paris is hosting the first-ever retrospective dedicated to the filmmaker, featuring all her films, a masterclass and a conversation with actor Adèle Haenel.

headline: The health of French presidents, a state secret
description: A new book unveils the last mysteries surrounding the illnesses of French presidents, especially that of Georges Pompidou (1969-1974), drawing on the diary of one of the president's physicians, Jean Bernard.

Bayeux Tapestry's transport to British Museum will be safe, detailed study determines
description: Before the fragile 70-meter-long embroidery is transported to the United Kingdom in July, a new risk assessment study released on Wednesday has given the move the green light.

headline: Edgar Morin: 'I doubt humanity even as I believe in it'
description: Over a series of conversations held between 2024 and 2026 with Le Monde, the philosopher and WWII resistant analyzed the contemporary world's upheaval, from the rise of authoritarianism in the West to conflicts in the Middle East, as well as the impact of new technologies and environmental issues.

headline: The 'palace' paradox: Most of France's top luxury hotels are owned by foreigners
description: The government's latest 'Palaces de France' list was revealed on June 2, with six new additions and four removals. The label aims to showcase this flagship sector of French expertise, even though the establishments' owners are rarely French.
